originalBehavior: The opening circle demo animates a point around the circumference, highlights radius, circumference, and diameter, and lets readers drag the point to resize the circle.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published MainCtrl and circle-demo directive, including the drag behavior, radius and diameter coupling, and animated circumference sweep.
notes: The live demo derives diameter from the current radius and rewrites opts.pos onto the circle on every size change.
The public route only changes surrounding shell and metadata.
evidence: Live source verified 2026-03-14 still exposes the circle-demo custom element and shared opts state.
Smoke coverage checks the SVG renders and that changing size updates the geometry labels and path state.

originalBehavior: The lower demo folds and unfolds a polygonal approximation of the circumference while keeping the diameter guide, circumference guide, and π ratio display synchronized.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published pi-demo directive and its wrap slider, using the same circumference path generation and label updates.
notes: The live page labels this control as wrap and pairs it with a separate size slider.
The polygon segment count stays fixed at the published value of 64 for v1.
evidence: Live source verified 2026-03-14 still binds ng-model="opts.fold" to the unwrap scene.
Smoke coverage changes fold locally and checks the path data changes with it.

originalBehavior: Changing the circle size updates the diameter and circumference labels while keeping the displayed π ratio constant.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published diameter watcher and label text generation so D and C update together as size changes.
notes: The public route uses a cleaned local control layout, but the underlying ng-model and redraw logic are unchanged.
The π text stays the published fixed approximation in the rendered fraction label.
evidence: Live source verified 2026-03-14 still binds ng-model="opts.diameter" to both demos.
Smoke coverage changes size locally and checks the D and C labels update together.

originalBehavior: The published page included EV branding, author bylines, share widgets, newsletter prompts, analytics, and comments around the explainer.
localStatus: The local route removes those public-shell elements and uses the shared footer as the only original-page reference surface.
notes: Technical provenance remains in docs only.
The top menu now routes back to the local replica index.
evidence: Public-surface auditing blocks EV branding, creator promo, share widgets, newsletter embeds, analytics, and comments from shipping routes.
The local page keeps a single shared-footer reference link to the live original.
